MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as
of the date last signed below (the "Effective Date") by and between Apex
Ventures, Inc., a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business
at 2400 Market Street, Suite 800, San Francisco, California 94114 ("Apex"),
and the counterparty identified on the signature page below ("Counterparty").
Apex and Counterparty are each a "Party" and together the "Parties." In
connection with a specific business opportunity between the Parties (the
"Purpose"), each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential,
proprietary, or non-public information, and the Parties wish to protect that
information on the terms set forth below.

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all information disclosed by
either Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving
Party"), whether disclosed orally, visually, in writing, in electronic form,
by demonstration, or in any other form or medium, that is or reasonably
should be understood to be confidential, proprietary, or of competitive
value, including without limitation: (a) business plans, strategies,
forecasts, budgets, customer lists, pricing, and financial information;
(b) product roadmaps, source code, object code, algorithms, designs, and
technical data; (c) research, know-how, trade secrets, inventions, and
unpublished patent applications; (d) personnel information and compensation
data; (e) the existence, content, and status of the Parties' discussions and
this Agreement itself; and (f) any information of a third party that is in
the possession of the Disclosing Party and subject to a duty of
confidentiality. Confidential Information need not be marked "Confidential"
to be protected hereunder.

1.2 "Representatives" means, with respect to a Party, its directors,
officers, employees, contractors, attorneys, accountants, and advisors who
have a legitimate need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF THE RECEIVING PARTY

2.1 The Receiving Party shall (a) use the Confidential Information solely
for the Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever; (b) protect the
Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care it uses to
protect its own confidential information of similar importance, but in no
event less than a reasonable degree of care; and (c) not disclose the
Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who
are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those set
forth in this Agreement.

2.2 The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this
Agreement by any of its Representatives and shall take all reasonable
measures to restrain its Representatives from any prohibited or unauthorized
disclosure or use of Confidential Information.

3. EXCEPTIONS

3.1 The obligations set forth in Section 2 shall not apply to information
that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written evidence: (a)
was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or subsequently entered
the public domain through no act or omission of the Receiving Party; (b) was
already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure, free of any
obligation of confidentiality; or (c) was received from a third party not
under an obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party.

4. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE

4.1 If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or
valid court or governmental order to disclose any Confidential Information,
the Receiving Party shall (a) promptly notify the Disclosing Party in
writing so the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other
appropriate remedy; (b) cooperate reasonably with the Disclosing Party in
any such effort; and (c) disclose only that portion of the Confidential
Information that is legally required to be disclosed. The Receiving Party
shall bear its own costs (including attorneys' fees) associated with any
such compelled disclosure and any objection or protective-order proceedings
it may undertake, regardless of which Party initiates or benefits from such
proceedings.

5. OWNERSHIP AND IMPROVEMENTS

5.1 All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the
Disclosing Party. No license, express or implied, to any intellectual
property right is granted by this Agreement other than the limited right to
use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

5.2 To the extent the Receiving Party generates any improvements, feedback,
suggestions, derivative works, or modifications relating to the
Confidential Information (collectively, "Improvements"), the Receiving
Party hereby irrevocably assigns to the Disclosing Party all right, title,
and interest in and to such Improvements, including all intellectual
property rights therein. The Receiving Party shall execute any documents
reasonably necessary to perfect such assignment.

6. TERM AND SURVIVAL

6.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in
full force and effect with respect to each item of Confidential
Information perpetually from the date of its disclosure. The Receiving
Party's obligations of confidentiality and non-use shall survive any
termination of discussions between the Parties and shall continue
indefinitely.

7. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION

7.1 Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party at any time, the
Receiving Party shall promptly (and in any event within ten (10) business
days) return or, at the Disclosing Party's option, destroy all Confidential
Information in its possession or control (including any copies, summaries,
or extracts in any form) and provide a written certification of destruction
signed by an officer of the Receiving Party.

8. NON-SOLICITATION

8.1 For a period of twenty-four (24) months from the Effective Date, the
Receiving Party shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or
engagement any employee or independent contractor of the Disclosing Party
with whom the Receiving Party had contact in connection with the Purpose,
nor shall the Receiving Party solicit business from any customer of the
Disclosing Party whose identity was learned as a result of the Confidential
Information.

9. REMEDIES

9.1 The Parties acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach of this
Agreement may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be
inadequate. Accordingly, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek
injunctive and other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a
bond or proving actual damages, in addition to any other remedies available
at law or in equity. The prevailing Party in any action to enforce this
Agreement shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and
costs from the other Party.

10. NO REPRESENTATIONS

10.1 ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND THE DISCLOSING
PARTY MAKES NO REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WITH
RESPECT TO ITS ACCURACY OR COMPLETENESS.

11. AMENDMENTS

11.1 Apex may amend or update this Agreement from time to time by posting
an updated version at www.apexventures.example/legal/nda or by providing
written or electronic notice to the Counterparty. Continued disclosure or
receipt of Confidential Information following such posting or notice shall
constitute acceptance of the updated Agreement. No amendment by the
Counterparty shall be effective unless agreed to in writing and signed by an
authorized officer of Apex.

12. GOVERNING LAW AND FORUM

12.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with
the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict-of-laws
principles. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state
and federal courts located in San Francisco County, California, for any
dispute arising under this Agreement, and waive any objection to venue in
those courts.

13. MISCELLANEOUS

13.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties
regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements and
understandings, written or oral.

13.2 This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be
deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the
same instrument. Electronic signatures shall be deemed originals for all
purposes.

13.3 No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless
in writing signed by the waiving Party, and no failure or delay in enforcing
any right shall operate as a waiver.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the
Effective Date.

APEX VENTURES, INC.                     COUNTERPARTY

By: ____________________________        By: ____________________________
Name: __________________________        Name: __________________________
Title: _________________________        Title: _________________________
Date: __________________________        Date: __________________________
